justification: All 6 ACs implemented and tested. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED with separate test cases. AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns 'unknown', with tests verifying warn is called. AC5 uses floating-point division with toFixed(2) for correct rounding (199→1.99, 1000→10.00). AC6 handles both null and undefined customer_name. 16 tests cover all 6 ACs with explicit names. Logger is injected (not global). acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly tested with both FAILED and DECLINED inputs as separate cases
AC4 tests include negative case verifying warn is NOT called for known statuses
AC5 tested with values requiring proper rounding (199→1.99, 150→1.50)
AC6 covers undefined, null, and valid name — all three branches
Logger injected via parameter, not global — clean dependency injection
Edge cases beyond spec: case sensitivity (lowercase 'paid'), empty string status, zero amount
